questions.question: What type of text is 'Kathmandu'?
questions.answer: A travelogue.

questions.question: Why did the author go to Kathmandu?
questions.answer: As part of his journey from China to India.

questions.question: Who accompanied the author to the temples?
questions.answer: Mr Shah’s son and nephew.

questions.question: What sign was displayed outside Pashupatinath temple?
questions.answer: 'Entrance for the Hindus only'.

questions.question: What caused the 'febrile confusion' in Pashupatinath?
questions.answer: Crowds of priests, devotees, animals and tourists.

questions.question: What happened between two monkeys at the temple?
questions.answer: They started fighting.

questions.question: What ritual was taking place on the Bagmati river bank?
questions.answer: Cremation of a dead body.

questions.question: What belief is connected with the small shrine on the river bank?
questions.answer: When it emerges fully, the goddess will escape and Kaliyug will end.

questions.question: How does the author describe Baudhnath stupa?
questions.answer: A place of quietness and peace.

questions.question: What kind of shops surrounded Baudhnath?
questions.answer: Small shops selling Tibetan items.

questions.question: What items were commonly sold there?
questions.answer: Felt bags, Tibetan prints and silver jewellery.

questions.question: What sounds filled the streets of Kathmandu?
questions.answer: Film songs, horns, bells and vendors’ cries.

questions.question: What sweets did the author buy?
questions.answer: Marzipan.

questions.question: Why did the author feel better after eating and drinking?
questions.answer: Because he relaxed after wandering the streets.

questions.question: Which travel route did the author consider before flying home?
questions.answer: Travelling by bus and train through Patna and Benaras.

questions.question: Why did the author not choose the long travel route?
questions.answer: He was tired and homesick.

questions.question: Where was the flute seller standing?
questions.answer: In a corner of the square near the hotel.

questions.question: What did the flute seller carry?
questions.answer: A pole with many flutes attached.

questions.question: How did the flute seller behave differently from other hawkers?
questions.answer: He quietly played music instead of shouting.

questions.question: Why does the author admire the flute seller?
questions.answer: Because of his calm and meditative playing.

questions.question: What does the author say about the universality of the flute?
questions.answer: Every culture has its own type of flute.

questions.question: Why is flute music special to the author?
questions.answer: It resembles the human voice.

questions.question: What emotional effect does the flute music have on the author?
questions.answer: It deeply moves him.

questions.question: What does the author realise after hearing the flute?
questions.answer: Music connects all human cultures.

questions.question: What major contrast does the lesson highlight?
questions.answer: The chaos of Pashupatinath and the peace of Baudhnath.
